properties.description:
Sailor Bar.
I've developed an interest in our local Eriogonum and came to realize the species found at Sailor Bar is E. roseum, not E. elongatum.  The latter does not occur naturally in our area.  It comes north in the coastal range to Monterey Co., only (Sources: Jepson and Calflora).  I have gone back on 9/7 to the exact same individual in this observation, taken measurements of the involucre, and added them here.

properties.description:
Have been searching for an example to ascertain this species during the past year and have finally found specimens with the required tiny hairy tufts at the junctions of the veins on the undersides of the leaflets (second and third photos demonstrate these in macro photos). The vast majority of walnuts here are old nonnative cultivars and hybrids (which lack this feature). 
Sailor Bar, Fair Oaks

properties.description:
Squared stems. Rough leaves are heart shaped to nearly round 
roughly 2/5 to 1" long, sparsely hairy and round-toothed.  Middle and upper leaves attached close to stem bases in opposing pairs. Lower leaves have stalks. Flowers emerge in whorls from clusters near base of leaves.  Flowers are slender, about 1/2-3/4" long, deep throated, with reddish dots on lower petals. 

Many newly emergent plants found in shady areas along equestrian trail.

Also called Giraffe's Head Henbit

properties.description:
This large, somewhat unhealthy looking tree clung to the edge of a steep embankment leading down to the river.  It was tall with drooping irregularly placed branches.  The branches still held some clusters of dried brown leaves from last season. 
Leaves were deeply lobed, 2-4" long.  The bark showed the characteristic thick, ridged pewter colored bark of valley oaks.  

The tree was beginning to bloom on all branches evidenced by the presence of fuzzy greenish yellow buds (that will eventually become catkins).

Valley Oak is an endemic species

properties.description:
This photo shows the bud end of a branch of a small 2' seedling.  Leave are enlongated ovals with several points along the edges. Leaves are thick, shiny, medium to dark green.  Similar trees in the area were in full leaf indicating they are evergreen. Leaves emerged in opposing pairs on short stem.  Next lower pair was about 1/4 turn around branch. 

This seedling was seen near the equestrian trail.  Many full size trees were in the area, with a wide canopy and reaching 20-30'.

properties.description:
Specimen was observed in the high water gravel field along the American River at Sailor Bar, about 15 yards from the boat ramp.  The plant lay close to the ground in a rosette pattern and was about 8" across. The longest leaves were at the base (about 4" long) and the smallest at the center (less than an inch).  The surface was woolly and soft on top and underside.  

There were no other specimens in the immediate area.

Illinois Ave, Gold River, CA, US

properties.description:
Long delicate flowers bloom in a tower on one side of a hairy stem, 10-20 per stem. Flowers are deep throated, rose pink near the stem and lavender purple at the open end. Flowers have a pea look with the upper petals a darker lavender and the lower petals a pale lavender.  Leaves line up in pairs along a long stem and are long and pointed, larger near the stem and smaller at the frond end, about 10 pair per stem.   

Plant lay along the ground and scrambled up the rock pile. 

Observed along equestrian trail not far from the boat ramp parking lot in an open area.

I understand this plant can be intentionally planted as a cover crop and is considered invasive.  I have seen it frequently in open fields and along roadways throughout greater Sacramento area.
